response:
<> To replace one URL with another within a string in PHP you can use the str_replace function. This function will work for repeating instances of the string as well. Here is an example of how you can achieve this ```php $string = 'New post on the site http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1

'; $oldUrl = 'http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1'; $newUrl = 'http://yahoo.com/abc1'; $replacedString = str_replace($oldUrl, $newUrl, $string); echo $replacedString; ``` This code will replace all occurrences of the old URL with the new URL in the given string. The output will be as expected A palindrome is a word phrase or sequence that reads the same backward as forward. An example of a palindrome is "madam" or "racecar"
